title: Early days of war against Iran boost satisfaction in Israel
subhead: Israel has continued to bombard Iran with massive, unrelenting waves of military aircraft, while also attacking Hezbollah in Lebanon. Its missile defense system has helped limit the damage on Israeli territory.

title: US says it is nearing 'complete control' of Iran airspace
subhead: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the US and Israel 'will have complete control of Iranian skies, uncontested airspace.' Trump, meanwhile, rated the US performance in the war with Iran as a '15' on 'a scale of 10,' saying the Islamic Republic's leaders were rapidly being killed and that Tehran's arsenal of ballistic missiles was also being 'wiped out.'

title: Sri Lanka recovers 87 bodies from Iranian warship sunk off its coast by a US submarine
subhead: The Iranian vessel was the Islamic Republic's 'prize ship,' according to the US defense secretary. This marks one of the few instances of a submarine sinking a ship since World War II.

title: For Lebanon's displaced, a bitter sense of déjà vu
subhead: The government reports that more than 58,000 people have fled their homes since the outbreak of the latest war between Israel and Hezbollah.

title: With the Strait of Hormuz blocked, regional trade disruptions could ripple across the globe
subhead: Following US-Israeli strikes on Iran, maritime traffic in the Persian Gulf has ground to a halt. These disruptions risk causing congestion at Asian ports.

subhead: The Bourse de Commerce unveils 'Clair-obscur,' an exhibition featuring around 20 artists who explore the space between shadow and light.

title: Iran's neighbors, under drone attacks, confront their vulnerability
subhead: Lacking experience in countering kamikaze drones, especially the infamous Shahed models, the Gulf countries have shown how unprepared they were for the threat posed by Iran, which is determined to exploit their vulnerability.

title: France's Iranian artistic diaspora torn between anxiety and joy
subhead: Since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, artists in the diaspora have found themselves dreaming of better days, even as they fear a rising death toll.
